subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The Spanish title precisely communicates the investigationâs focus, establishing clear purpose.
The research question is prominently displayed, narrowly focused, and consistently guides the report.
Methodology is comprehensive, with justified equipment selection (vacuum system, Arduino) and explicit assumptions (ideal-gas, isothermal).
weaknesses: Vacuum pump specifications (e.g., ultimate pressure) are not fully detailed, limiting exact reproducibility.
Controlled-variables table omits humidity measurement ranges, reducing control clarity.
Minor redundant phrasing in the introduction could be tightened for conciseness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the 5â6 band descriptors: topic and research question are communicated accurately and purposefully; methodology is complete with clear evidence of informed selection. The minor omissions noted do not prevent the report from fulfilling the highest band requirements, justifying full marks (6/6).

strengths: Derivations from first principles and conversion from dB to SI intensity show coherent, in-depth understanding.
Technical terminology (e.g., âcoeficiente adiabÃ¡ticoâ, âproceso isotÃ©rmicoâ) is accurate and consistently applied.
Well-structured equations and integrated background data (regional air composition) demonstrate effective application of subject knowledge.
weaknesses: A minor slip in the unit notation for the proportionality constant K was noted.
There is a mismatch between the theoretical and measured slope that could be more thoroughly explained.
Symbol inconsistency (v vs. Î½) appears in one section, which may confuse readers.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs use of physics concepts and terminology is consistently precise, and source application is highly relevant. Although small technical and notational issues exist, they do not detract from the overall excellence of the work. This warrants full marks (6/6) in Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Analysis is focused on the research question, with well-plotted data, error bars, linear regression and discussion of RÂ².
Arguments are logically developed from theory to method to data to conclusion, exhibiting clear coherence.
Evaluation demonstrates insight into theoretical assumptions, apparatus limitations, and proposes realistic improvements.
weaknesses: Quantitative depth could be enhanced with uncertainty propagation or statistical tests.
Instrument precision (e.g., vacuometer error) is not explicitly quantified.
Discussion of the ideal-gas assumption at low pressures could be expanded for deeper critical perspective.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs work demonstrates sophisticated research analysis, coherent argument development and an excellent level of critical evaluation throughout. While there is room for deeper quantitative critique, the quality and focus of thinking clearly meet the 10â12 band for Critical Thinking, meriting the maximum 12/12.

strengths: Report follows IB conventions with a contents page, labelled figures, sequential sections and clear formatting.
Visual elements (photographs, diagrams) are well-labelled and enhance comprehension of the setup.
Layout supports logical flow and readability, with original figure captions indicating student authorship.
weaknesses: The table of contents lacks accurate page numbers for some sections.
There are occasional encoding errors (e.g., accented characters) and duplicated images.
Some tables are overcrowded and could be simplified for better readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall structure and visual presentation are clear and effective, aligning with the 3â4 band descriptors. The minor formatting inconsistencies do not impede communication, justifying the full Presentation mark (4/4).

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical termsââimpedanciaâ, âcoeficiente adiabÃ¡ticoâ, âproceso isotÃ©rmicoâ, âincertidumbreââare used accurately and consistently; equations are formatted and referenced. Isolated typographical artefacts do not undermine conceptual precision. This fits the top band for terminology.

The argument develops logically: theory â method â data â evaluation â conclusion. Connections between sections are explicit and the student weighs competing explanations (solid-path sound, humidity changes). Some reasoning is qualitative or speculative rather than supported by calculations, but coherence and structure remain strong, warranting an 8 within the 7-9 band.

The essay outlines variables, controls, detailed apparatus photographs, a step-by-step procedure and justification for equipment choice (vacuum range, Arduino data logging). Assumptions such as ideal-gas behaviour and isothermal conditions are made explicit, showing informed selection of methods. Weaknesses (limited pressure range, sound leakage) are recognised later but not fully mitigated, so the strand merits a solid 5 rather than 6.

The student derives relationships from first principles, converts dB to SI intensity, applies ideal-gas and acoustic equations and interprets RÂ². Physics understanding of impedance, adiabatic index and thermodynamics is coherent and extends beyond textbook recall. A few conceptual slips (unit on K constant, mismatch between theoretical and measured slope) keep the work just short of âconsistently excellentâ, hence 5.

Evaluation addresses theoretical assumptions, apparatus limitations and external variables, proposing realistic improvements. However, the critique is more descriptive than quantitative and does not revisit all sources of uncertainty in depth, so the strand sits at the lower end of the 7-9 band (7).

The student introduces the topic with a clear Spanish title, an English-style abstract sentence and an explicit research question printed in bold. The purposeâinvestigating how gas pressure influences acoustic intensityâis unambiguous and is restated in the introduction, conclusion and several section headings, showing a sustained focus. Minor language glitches (accent corruption, âprovectosâ) do not impede meaning. Overall communication is accurate, purposeful and logically directed toward the stated aim, matching the 5-6 band.

Diagrams and tables aid comprehension, captions are present and most visuals are referenced in the text. Image quality is adequate, alignment occasionally uneven, and bibliography formatting is inconsistent, so a competent but not flawless presentation earns 3 in the top band.

The research question is quoted verbatim, is sufficiently narrow (sound within a laboratory vacuum chamber) and is revisited in data analysis and the conclusion. Links between the RQ, hypothesis and data table/graph are explicit; the question clearly guides the structure of the report, satisfying the descriptors of a well-focused RQ in the top band, though minor wording redundancy prevents full marks.

Data are plotted with error bars; linear regression and RÂ² are calculated; discrepancies between theoretical and experimental K are discussed alongside multiple sources of error. Analysis stays focused on the RQ throughout. While thoughtful, it lacks the quantitative depth (e.g., propagated uncertainty on slope, statistical tests) required for the highest 10-12 band, so 7-9 band with a mid-high score of 8 is appropriate.

The essay contains a contents page, labelled figures, sequential page sections and complies with IB structural conventions. There are occasional encoding errors and duplicated images, yet overall organisation is clear, matching the 3-4 band; minor flaws justify 3 rather than 4.

annotations.comment: Note that Ec.3 assumes constant temperature; discuss how temperature control (e.g., bath equilibration time) was validated experimentally.
The Spanish title is precise and clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on vacuum pressure and sound intensity, establishing a strong foundation for the reportâs purpose.
The inclusion of a word count at the document start meets IB formatting requirements, demonstrating adherence to guidelines.
The research question is prominently displayed and narrowly focused on impedance and intensity in a vacuum chamber, effectively guiding the studyâs structure.
Typographical errors such as âaguÃ­â and âprovectosâ distract from clarity. Proofread for spelling and accentuation to uphold professional tone.
Consider tightening the introduction by reducing redundant phrasing (e.g., linking future projects) to enhance focus and conciseness.
The table of contents lacks page numbers for most sections, making navigation difficult. Include accurate pagination to improve usability.
The introduction reiterates the research question effectively and outlines the investigationâs aims, maintaining clear focus on pressureâintensity relationships.
Citing âLumitos 2024â for adiabatic coefficients is clear, but verify source authority and consider primary literature for rigor.
The vacuum pump description lacks specific operational parameters (e.g., ultimate pressure), limiting reproducibility. Include pump specifications.
The foundational description of wave behavior is clear but could be enhanced by early inclusion of key acoustic equations linking pressure and intensity.
Definitions of amplitude and frequency are accurate and supported by citation, demonstrating solid understanding of key concepts.
The derivation of $$v=\sqrt{\tfrac{\gamma RT}{M}}$$ is well presented, integrating elastic modulus and density effectively.
Table 1 is overcrowded and many cells are empty, hindering readability. Simplify or split into smaller tables to clarify data presentation.
Images of the vacuum chamber and pump are clear and well labeled, aiding comprehension of the experimental setup.
The figure captions specify âElaboraciÃ³n propia,â reflecting originality in apparatus design.
The notation switches from \(v\) to \(\nu\) in equations, which may confuse readers; maintain consistent symbols.
Add a brief discussion of the limitations of the idealâgas assumption at low pressures to critically evaluate the theoretical model.
Instrument precision (e.g., vacuometer error) is omitted. Specify device uncertainty to strengthen data validity.
The derivation of $$v=\sqrt{\tfrac{\gamma PV}{m}}$$ from the idealâgas law is clear and logically structured.
The algebraic steps in deriving Ec.3 are very dense; consider numbering each step and referencing them to improve readability.
The conclusion that intensity is directly proportional to pressure (I=PK) is well stated, reinforcing the theoretical link.
In the controlledâvariables table, humidity HR is not quantified; specify measurement method or expected variation range to strengthen control.
Distance dââáµ£ lacks units in the table; include millimeters or centimeters to avoid ambiguity.
The regional air composition table is well integrated, showing appropriate application of background data.
Abundance percentages lack stated uncertainty; discuss how variation in composition may impact the calculated molar mass.
